selected_goal: Extend the current output-integrity audit so it reports suspicious rendered crop dimensions for question and mark-scheme PNGs.
why_this_now: The previous governor identified corpus-level suspicious rendered-crop detection as the next priority after fixing isolated historical crop leaks. A dimension gate is the smallest reusable first step.
repo_evidence: .agent-runs/2026-06-19T02-56-31-928Z/governor-review.json recommends detecting tall or multi-anchor rendered crops before further isolated parser refinements.
src/exam_bank/audit.py already owns the fail-fast output-integrity audit and resolves question/mark-scheme image artifact paths.
.agent-loop/project-gates.md lists output-integrity-audit as the relevant generated-artifact gate.
acceptance_criteria: The audit inspects readable PNG dimensions for all resolved question and mark-scheme image paths without rewriting artifacts.
A very tall or extreme-aspect rendered crop creates a deterministic failure group with record, field, path, dimensions, and reason evidence.
Existing fake-file tests continue to pass by treating unreadable-but-existing images as outside this dimension check rather than crashing.
test_expectations: Add a targeted test in tests/test_audit.py using small generated PNGs that should fail the new suspicious-dimension check.
Run the targeted audit tests after implementation.
implementation_boundaries: Do not add a new dependency; use Pillow, which is already a project dependency.
Do not regenerate canonical output or change renderer behavior in this iteration.
Do not convert suspicious dimensions into clean/ready statuses; only report them through the audit.
repo_hygiene_risk: Low. The change is limited to one existing audit module, one test file, and run-local artifacts.
rollback_plan: Remove the new dimension check and its targeted test; no generated output state is modified.
done_when: The targeted test passes, existing current-output-integrity tests still pass, and the audit artifact records a pass or pass_with_risks verdict.
